refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability ≥ 0.999 (99.9%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the bidirectional command-and-telemetry link is operational to total evaluation time.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-010 addresses a critical operational requirement: remote equipment control depends on reliable communication. The refined FR quantifies the availability target, specifies bidirectionality (command uplink + telemetry downlink), and anchors measurement to an agreed evaluation period. This is essential for safety-critical teleoperation where link loss can lead to loss of control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous monitoring of link state (up/down) during evaluation period; log all link transitions; calculate availability = (total_time - downtime) / total_time; compare against 0.999 threshold. Acceptance requires independent verification by Safety Manager and Site Manager.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-010.1: The teleoperation system shall establish and maintain a bidirectional command uplink from tele-operator to vehicle.
FR-NGHE-010.2: The teleoperation system shall establish and maintain a bidirectional telemetry downlink from vehicle to tele-operator.
FR-NGHE-010.3: The teleoperation system shall detect link loss and initiate safe state transition within defined timeout.
FR-NGHE-010.4: The teleoperation system shall provide real-time link quality metrics to the tele-operator and Safety Manager.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Redundant network paths (primary + backup) with automatic failover; dual-modem architecture; geographic diversity of base stations.
DP-B: Single high-reliability network link with enhanced error correction and retransmission; QoS prioritization; link monitoring and predictive maintenance.
DP-C: Hybrid approach: primary link with secondary backup; active health monitoring; automatic switchover on link degradation; network abstraction layer to support multiple carrier technologies.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid redundancy with active health monitoring and automatic failover. Implement dual-path architecture (primary + secondary network link) with continuous link quality assessment; trigger automatic switchover when primary link quality degrades below threshold; provide network abstraction layer to support multiple carrier technologies (cellular, satellite, private network); integrate link state telemetry into Safety Services for real-time operator awareness.

potential_sources: DP-C couples teleoperation link availability to network infrastructure selection (carrier, technology, coverage), which is a design decision but not a functional coupling—the DP remains independent of specific carrier choice.
DP-C couples to Safety Services for link state reporting, but this is a logical dependency (not a physical constraint) and is properly managed via the SafetyAlert interface.
DP-C couples to Vehicle for dual-modem hardware integration, but this is expected and managed via clear interface specification (Tasking in, Telemetry out).
coupling_check.notes: No problematic functional couplings detected. DP-C is decoupled from specific network technology, allowing flexibility in carrier selection. Coupling to Safety Services is intentional and necessary for safety-critical operation.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Secondary network link (backup) is available and can be provisioned within project timeline and budget.
ASSUMPTION: Network abstraction layer can be implemented to support seamless switching between carrier technologies without loss of command/telemetry continuity.
RISK: Automatic failover logic may introduce latency during switchover; if switchover time exceeds safe state transition timeout, vehicle may enter uncontrolled state.
RISK: Dual-modem hardware adds cost and power consumption; may impact vehicle design and operational economics.
RISK: Link quality thresholds for triggering failover must be carefully tuned; too aggressive triggers false failovers; too conservative misses real degradation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly specify: (1) failover latency budget (e.g., <500ms), (2) link quality metrics and thresholds (e.g., signal strength, packet loss rate, round-trip latency), (3) safe state transition procedure during switchover, (4) network abstraction layer API and supported technologies. Add acceptance criterion: failover must complete within latency budget without loss of command/telemetry continuity.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Owns the teleoperation link availability function, including dual-path network management, link health monitoring, automatic failover logic, and link state reporting. Responsible for command uplink and telemetry downlink continuity. Interfaces with Vehicle (for modem hardware and telemetry sources), Safety Services (for link state alerts), and external Network Link (for carrier connectivity).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Tele-operator commands (movement, tool control, etc.) received from remote control station; transmitted to Vehicle via primary or secondary network link.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle state, sensor data, and link quality metrics transmitted from Vehicle to tele-operator; includes link health indicators (signal strength, latency, packet loss) for operator awareness.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Link state transitions (loss, degradation, recovery) reported to Safety Services for real-time safety monitoring and operator notification.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication with external network infrastructure (primary and secondary carrier networks); Teleoperation module manages link selection, failover, and quality monitoring.

module_containment_scores.notes: Teleoperation is the natural owner of link availability function. Contains dual-path management, failover logic, and link monitoring. Minimal diffusion: only external interface to Network Link (expected) and SafetyAlert reporting (intentional coupling for safety).

Vehicle owns modem hardware and telemetry sources, but should NOT own link availability logic. Assigning DP-C to Vehicle would diffuse failover logic across vehicle firmware, creating tight coupling to network technology and complicating vehicle design. Vehicle should be a passive provider of telemetry and command execution.

Safety Services owns safety monitoring and alerts, but should NOT own link availability function. Assigning DP-C to Safety Services would create circular dependency: Safety Services depends on link availability to report link state. Safety Services should consume link state information, not produce it.

NGHE Solution is the system-level container, not an implementation module. Assigning DP-C to NGHE Solution would leave the function unowned at the module level, diffusing responsibility across multiple sub-systems.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Teleoperation module is the correct owner of link availability function. It naturally contains dual-path network management, link health monitoring, and failover logic. It has clear interfaces to Vehicle (for modem hardware), Safety Services (for link state alerts), and Network Link (for carrier connectivity). Containment score of 0.95 reflects minimal diffusion and high cohesion.
containment_risks: RISK: Teleoperation module may become too large if it also owns network protocol implementation (e.g., custom compression, encryption). Recommend separating network protocol layer into a distinct sub-module or library.

RISK: Failover logic must be deterministic and testable; if Teleoperation module couples failover to specific carrier APIs, it becomes difficult to switch carriers. Recommend network abstraction layer to decouple failover from carrier-specific details.

RISK: Link quality thresholds and failover parameters may need tuning based on field data; if these are hardcoded in Teleoperation module, updates require firmware release. Recommend parameterization via configuration file or remote update mechanism.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm Teleoperation module ownership. Refine module scope to explicitly exclude network protocol implementation (delegate to sub-module or library). Define network abstraction layer API to decouple failover logic from carrier-specific details. Implement link quality parameters as configurable (not hardcoded) to enable field tuning without firmware release.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid redundancy with active health monitoring) directly addresses FR requirement for ≥0.999 link availability. DP-C provides dual-path architecture to eliminate single points of failure, active monitoring to detect degradation early, and automatic failover to maintain continuity. DP-C is implementable, testable, and measurable against the 0.999 threshold. No functional gaps identified.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK: If Teleoperation module also owns network protocol implementation, it becomes tightly coupled to carrier-specific APIs and difficult to switch carriers. MITIGATION: Separate network protocol layer into distinct sub-module with clean abstraction.

DIFFUSION RISK: If failover logic is hardcoded with link quality thresholds, field tuning requires firmware release. MITIGATION: Parameterize thresholds via configuration file or remote update mechanism.

DIFFUSION RISK: If Vehicle module is required to implement failover logic (e.g., handling link loss on vehicle side), responsibility becomes diffused. MITIGATION: Clarify that Vehicle is passive provider of telemetry; Teleoperation owns failover logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module is the correct owner with containment score 0.95. Clear interfaces to Vehicle, Safety Services, and Network Link. Minimal diffusion if network abstraction layer is properly implemented. Recommend sub-module decomposition for network protocol layer to further reduce diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Link availability is directly measurable via continuous monitoring of link state (up/down) during evaluation period. Acceptance criterion (≥0.999) is quantitative and verifiable. Teleoperation module can provide link state telemetry and logs for independent verification by Safety Manager and Site Manager.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-010 is clearly allocated to Teleoperation module. Sub-FRs (command uplink, telemetry downlink, link loss detection, link quality metrics) are all contained within Teleoperation scope. No ambiguity in allocation or ownership.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to specify failover latency budget (e.g., <500ms) and link quality thresholds (e.g., signal strength, packet loss rate, round-trip latency).
Define network abstraction layer API to decouple failover logic from carrier-specific details.
Implement link quality parameters as configurable (not hardcoded) to enable field tuning.
Clarify safe state transition procedure during failover to ensure vehicle enters safe state if failover latency exceeds timeout.
Specify acceptance test procedure: continuous link monitoring during evaluation period, calculation of availability metric, independent verification by Safety Manager and Site Manager.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-010 is not atomic; it decomposes into four sub-FRs (command uplink, telemetry downlink, link loss detection, link quality metrics). Each sub-FR requires further refinement and DP selection. DP-C is sound and well-allocated to Teleoperation module, but implementation details (failover latency, link quality thresholds, network abstraction layer) must be refined at the next decomposition level. Recommend proceeding to next level to decompose sub-FRs and detail DP-C implementation.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-010.1: The teleoperation system shall establish and maintain a bidirectional command uplink from tele-operator to vehicle with latency ≤ [TBD] ms.
FR-NGHE-010.2: The teleoperation system shall establish and maintain a bidirectional telemetry downlink from vehicle to tele-operator with latency ≤ [TBD] ms.
FR-NGHE-010.3: The teleoperation system shall detect link loss and initiate safe state transition within [TBD] ms timeout.

FR-NGHE-010.4: The teleoperation system shall provide real-time link quality metrics (signal strength, packet loss rate, round-trip latency) to the tele-operator and Safety Manager with update frequency ≥ [TBD] Hz.

revise.why: DP-C requires refinement to specify: (1) failover latency budget, (2) link quality metrics and thresholds, (3) safe state transition procedure, (4) network abstraction layer API. These details are necessary to guide implementation and enable verification at the next decomposition level.

assumptions: Secondary network link (backup) is available and can be provisioned within project timeline and budget.
Network abstraction layer can be implemented to support seamless switching between carrier technologies without loss of command/telemetry continuity.
Link quality thresholds can be tuned based on field data without requiring firmware release (parameterized configuration).
Safe state transition procedure is defined and can be executed within failover latency budget.
Evaluation period is sufficient to measure 0.999 availability (e.g., 30 days minimum to capture rare failure modes).
open_risks: Automatic failover logic may introduce latency during switchover; if switchover time exceeds safe state transition timeout, vehicle may enter uncontrolled state. MITIGATION: Define failover latency budget and safe state transition procedure; validate via testing.

Dual-modem hardware adds cost and power consumption; may impact vehicle design and operational economics. MITIGATION: Conduct cost-benefit analysis; consider phased deployment (single modem in Phase 1, dual-modem in Phase 2).

Link quality thresholds must be carefully tuned; too aggressive triggers false failovers; too conservative misses real degradation. MITIGATION: Parameterize thresholds; conduct field tuning during evaluation period.

Network abstraction layer may introduce performance overhead or complexity; difficult to support multiple carrier technologies. MITIGATION: Prototype network abstraction layer early; validate performance and carrier support.

Safety criticality (high) requires rigorous validation of failover logic and safe state transition; may require formal verification or extensive testing. MITIGATION: Allocate resources for safety validation; consider third-party safety assessment.
evidence_needed: Failover latency measurement: Conduct lab testing to measure failover latency under various network conditions (signal loss, congestion, etc.); validate against latency budget.
Link quality threshold tuning: Conduct field testing during evaluation period to measure link quality metrics under real-world conditions; tune thresholds based on observed data.
Safe state transition validation: Conduct testing to verify that vehicle enters safe state within timeout during link loss; validate across all operational scenarios.
Network abstraction layer validation: Prototype network abstraction layer; validate performance, carrier support, and seamless switching between technologies.
Availability measurement: Conduct continuous monitoring during evaluation period; calculate availability metric; validate against 0.999 threshold.
Safety validation: Conduct safety analysis (FMEA, FTA) for failover logic and link loss scenarios; validate against safety requirements; consider third-party safety assessment.
